# airtelcloud_vpc_peering (Resource)

Manages an Airtel Cloud VPC Peering connection.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `az` (String) The availability zone for the VPC peering connection.
- `name` (String) The name of the VPC peering connection.
- `region` (String) The region for the VPC peering connection.

### Optional

- `allowed_subnet_list` (List of String) List of allowed subnet IDs for the peering connection.
- `blocked_subnet_list` (List of String) List of blocked subnet IDs for the peering connection.
- `description` (String) A description of the VPC peering connection.
- `is_pcl_enabled` (Boolean) Whether PCL (Private Connectivity Link) is enabled.
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))
- `vpc_source_id` (String) The ID of the source VPC. Either vpc_source_id or vpc_source_name must be specified.
- `vpc_source_name` (String) The name of the source VPC. Either vpc_source_id or vpc_source_name must be specified.
- `vpc_target_id` (String) The ID of the target VPC. Either vpc_target_id or vpc_target_name must be specified.
- `vpc_target_name` (String) The name of the target VPC. Either vpc_target_id or vpc_target_name must be specified.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the VPC peering connection.
- `state` (String) The current state of the VPC peering connection.

<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
